compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Why

The plan-review `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecar is the only durable record of a review's four passes, but
its `_slim` step drops the pass-1 `evidence` and `scenarios` fields, and it does not persist the
numeric `block_threshold`/`blocking_enabled` each finding was judged against (these are resolved
from the criteria registry at decision time and then discarded). Without them an auditor cannot see
a finding's grounding quotes or the exact decision boundary that was applied.

## What

Extend the plan-review sidecar to a new schema version `plan_review_result_v2` that additionally
persists, per finding: the pass-1 `evidence[]` and `scenarios[]`, and the resolved `block_threshold`
(float) and `blocking_enabled` (bool) that pass-3 used. All existing fields and the all-buckets
pooling (blocking + advisory + overflow + indeterminate + dropped) are unchanged.

## Scope

- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py` — bump `SCHEMA` to `plan_review_result_v2`; stop stripping
  `evidence`/`scenarios` in `_slim`; thread the resolved `block_threshold`/`blocking_enabled` from
  the pass-3 decision into each persisted finding; `latest_review_result` accepts both `v1` and `v2`.
- `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py` — expose the resolved `block_threshold`/`blocking_enabled`
  on each finding's decision output (the `ThresholdResolver` result is currently used then dropped).

## Approach rationale

Bump the schema and widen the payload rather than write a parallel record, so the single existing
emit/prune/read path carries the richer data unchanged in shape. The resolved threshold is captured
at the exact point pass-3 applies it, which is the only place it is knowable without re-reading the
registry (avoiding historical drift).

## Compatibility & rollback

`v1` sidecars remain valid and are read with the new fields absent (readers treat missing
threshold/evidence as "not recorded"). No backfill. Reverting restores the `v1` payload; already-
written `v2` events remain readable by the tolerant reader. Adding fields to the payload does not
change the reducer-ignored status or the event type, so older clients keep skipping the event.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Running `review-plan` emits a `REVIEW_RESULT` event whose `schema` is `plan_review_result_v2`
      and whose every persisted finding carries `evidence`, `scenarios`, `block_threshold`, and
      `blocking_enabled`. Oracle: an in-process integration test drives a review with a stub LLM
      runner and asserts on the emitted event JSON via the real sidecar emit path (test-design §3
      producer/consumer shape).
- [ ] `pass3_decide`/`pass3_over_findings` expose the resolved `block_threshold`/`blocking_enabled`
      on each finding. Oracle: a unit test against the real decide functions asserting the fields
      are present with the resolved values.
- [ ] `latest_review_result` reads both a `v1` and a `v2` sidecar file without error, reporting the
      new fields absent for `v1`. Oracle: a unit test feeding one hand-written `v1` and one `v2`
      event.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass.

file_impact.reason: enrich payload to v2 + tolerant reader

compiled_state.title: plan-review sidecar -> lossless v2 (evidence, scenarios, thresholds)
